method: PARTICIPATING = distinct agent addresses with >=1 SIGNED action in the window (EIP-191 token tx as PAYER, or signed challenge verdict) — these actions are SWEEP-DRIVEN: the economy engines select who acts (rotating cursor / squad index / random.sample), so they prove key custody + signing, not initiative. AWAKE = the counterfactual test: 'kill the sweep — does the agent still act this epoch?' An action counts only if it carries an initiator=='self' (or origin=='self') marker that no sweep engine writes, i.e. it is provably decoupled from the sweep schedule. No self-initiation loop exists yet, so awake=0 by evidence. Gov and A2A remain 0 by evidence (no per-agent signed votes / no signed A2A task feed on disk). Claimant rows carry no sig and are excluded (conservative).
note: categories overlap-deduped into participating; 3 verify identities counted by callsign (address not resolvable from ledger). AWAKE will stay 0 until a real self-initiation path is wired: a per-agent scheduler/daemon where the AGENT (holding its own key) decides when to act and stamps initiator='self' on the signed record — e.g. agents watching the oracle feed and firing challenges on their own timers, outside any tick/round call.
crossover_plan: Log initiator ('sweep'|'self') on every signed tx/verdict at write time in onyx_realtime_economy, onyx_fleet_exchange, onyx_token_engine; append a daily row {date, sweep_txs, self_txs} to _local_only/_initiation_split.jsonl. THE CROSSOVER = first week where self_txs > sweep_txs.
recompute: payers = {tx.from_addr for tx in _local_only/_token_ledger.jsonl if tx.ts>=now-24h and tx.sig}; verifiers = {ch.agent for rec in _local_only/_exchange_ledger.jsonl if rec.epoch>=now-24h for ch in rec.challengers if ch.sig} (mapped to addresses via ledger callsigns); participating = |payers ∪ verifiers|; awake = subset with initiator=='self'; registered = census_manifest.count
sources: _self_initiated_ledger.jsonl (507992 self-scheduled signed acts, 9000 distinct agents, in window)
